key_insight: ARIA reduces LLM "contextual tunneling" by routing materials queries through causal reasoning, analogical transfer, or parametric fallback based on how complete the Process-Structure-Property evidence is.
engineering_relevance: The framework improves trustworthy forward prediction and inverse design for materials discovery by producing physically grounded, auditable reasoning instead of over-relying on retrieved text snippets.
method: Build a knowledge graph from literature-extracted Process-Structure-Property relations.
Use a three-tier cascade: direct causal reasoning, physics-informed analogical transfer, or explicit parametric fallback.
Add evidence enrichment with online literature search to strengthen incomplete cases.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a KG with 2,839 extracted PSP relations and evaluation on forward prediction and inverse design for 2D materials, with ARIA outperforming unaugmented and naive KG-augmented baselines.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the completeness and quality of extracted literature evidence, so sparse or noisy knowledge graphs could still limit reliability.
paper_title: ARIA: A Causal-Aware Framework for Rescuing LLM Reasoning in Trustworthy Materials Discovery
